summary: 62 tests passed, 100.0% coverage, all quality gates passed

completion_promises.criterion_text: to_connect_kwargs() -> dict[str, Any] method added to NATSConfig — Returns {"servers": [self.url], "connect_timeout": self.connect_timeout, "reconnect_time_wait": self.reconnect_time_wait, "max_reconnect_attempts": self.max_reconnect_attempts, "name": self.name} — Includes "user": self.user, "password": self.password.get_secret_value() when both are set — Includes "credentials": self.creds_file when creds_file is set
completion_promises.evidence: Added to_connect_kwargs() method to NATSConfig (config.py lines 153-178). Method builds a dict with servers (as list), connect_timeout, reconnect_time_wait, max_reconnect_attempts, and name. Conditionally includes user+password (unwrapped from SecretStr) when both are set, and credentials when creds_file is set. Verified by 12 tests in TestToConnectKwargsSmoke, TestToConnectKwargsKeyExample, and TestToConnectKwargsEdgeCase classes — all passing.

completion_promises.criterion_text: model_dump() does not expose raw password string — SecretStr serialises as "**********" by default in pydantic v2
completion_promises.evidence: Verified by TestSerialisationMasking::test_model_dump_password_is_masked and pre-existing TestNATSConfigAuthBoundary::test_password_not_exposed_in_model_dump. Both confirm 'super-secret' does not appear in model_dump() output. This is pydantic v2 built-in SecretStr behaviour — no implementation changes needed, just test verification.

completion_promises.criterion_text: repr(config) / str(config) does not expose raw password value
completion_promises.evidence: Verified by TestSerialisationMasking::test_repr_password_is_masked and TestSerialisationMasking::test_str_password_is_masked. Both confirm 'super-secret' does not appear in repr() or str() output. Pre-existing test TestNATSConfigAuthBoundary::test_password_masked_in_repr also confirms this. Pydantic v2 SecretStr handles masking automatically.

completion_promises.criterion_text: to_connect_kwargs() output is accepted by nats-py Client.connect(**kwargs) (verified in integration test)
completion_promises.evidence: Added TestToConnectKwargsIntegration::test_kwargs_accepted_by_nats_client_connect marked with @pytest.mark.integration. The test inspects the Client.connect() signature and verifies all keys from to_connect_kwargs() are accepted parameters. Uses pytest.skip if nats-py is not installed. This does not require a running NATS server.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: Ran ruff check on both modified files (config.py and test_config.py) — 'All checks passed!' Ran mypy --strict on config.py — 'Success: no issues found in 1 source file'. Both files compile without errors via py_compile.
